text:
Once upon a time in a small town called Serenity Springs, there was an owl named Olive who loved nothing more than reading. Every night after owl hours, Olive would cozy up with a good book. One peculiar thing happened one autumn. The leaves turned ruby red, and rain fell velvet. A mysterious map appeared under an ancient oak tree—one that seemed to flutter with bats.

Curious, Olive followed it. When she finally came upon a cottage draped in midnight blue. There stood an old woman with twinkling stars instead of eyes.

"I hear you've found my map," she said warmly.

"Yes!" said Olive. "But I can't find anything."

The woman chuckled softly. "Follow me. There's something you seek."

Inside, there were chests full of feathers. It was like flying. She picked.

"The creature you find will tell you why you dream."

Olive picked quills. Nothing extraordinary. Then she picked feathers with little red dots.

"The wolf."

A wolf!
